channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: A New Hampshire company is working to develop a fleet of autonomous helicopters that it hopes can be used to put out fires, spray crops and handle other dangers jobs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: San Francisco’s mayor-elect picks Sam Altman to co-chair transition team
item.description: San Francisco Mayor-elect Daniel Lurie has picked OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to help run his transition team as he prepares to take office in January, Lurie said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Data-driven startup Aggie uses AI to manage small business social media
item.description: A new, AI-powered platform called Aggie wants to manage your brand’s social media. Aggie is a product of Audience Genomics, which has since 2018 offered audience data analytics to big brands like Universal Studios, AMC Television, and Fenty Beauty.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Yellowjackets is claimed to have ‘copied’ its concept from a thriller movie you can stream on Tubi
item.description: Yellowjackets is being sued for copying its plot from a 2015 thriller movie that's available to watch on Tubi.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Trump’s pick for FCC chairman is Brendan Carr
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Sunday named Brendan Carr, the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission, as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications, and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: From Quibi’s ashes, new short-form streamers are thriving
item.description: Four years after Quibi’s $1.75 billion crashout, its successors are flying high. Apps like DramaBox and ReelShort are betting on short-form. They present feature-length films, diced into consumable minutes-long bites akin to a TikTok. The more clips you want to see, the more you’ll have to pay.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Trump names Brendan Carr, senior GOP leader at FCC, to lead the agency
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has named the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats are missing the point on Joe Rogan
item.description: The weeks after an election are open season for pet political theories. An electorate as massive and sprawling as America’s will never swing on just one thing. But that’s no fun to admit. So why not share your one thing—you can be as right as anyone else.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bien-Kahn, Fast Company

item.title: Sophos Urgent Warning: Google Search Hijacked by Hackers | Stay Safe Online
item.description: Sophos warns against typing six specific words into search engines, as hackers exploit SEO tactics to steal personal info and install malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Onion CEO explains Infowars purchase amid Alex Jones's claims about auction
item.description: The Onion CEO Ben Collins confirmed that the publication purchased Alex Jones’s Infowars outlet in a Saturday series of posts on the social media platform Bluesky amid Jones's claims of wrongdoing and illegal activity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: What You Can Do to Maintain a Healthy Brain
item.description: Maintaining cognitive health through challenging activities, brain training exercises, and lifestyle changes.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s AirPods Pro 2 makes hearing tests as easy as a few taps
item.description: Checking your hearing has gotten easier with the new hearing test feature in AirPods Pro 2. You can take reliable hearing tests using your iPhone or iPad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawsuit alleging Elon Musk rigged Dogecoin is dropped
item.description: A lawsuit accusing Elon Musk of rigging Dogecoin is ending. Investors in the cryptocurrency who said the world’s richest person and his electric car company Tesla committed fraud and insider trading are withdrawing their appeal from an Aug. 29 dismissal of their case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AI is already taking jobs, research shows. Routine tasks are the first to go
item.description: Generative AI tools such as ChatGPT are already impacting the job market. A new study shows a 21% reduction in demand for freelance jobs that are automation-prone and don’t require consistent human attention.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Psaki: 'Disinformation space' on social media a 'core' issue behind Harris defeat
item.description: Former White House press secretary Jen Psaki is blaming Vice President Harris's loss to President-elect Trump in part on disinformation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 18 states sue SEC, Gensler for 'regulatory overreach' on crypto
item.description: Eighteen Republican attorneys general sued the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Chair Gary Gensler on Thursday for allegedly overstepping the agency’s authority in its enforcement actions against the cryptocurrency industry.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to use DispatchProxy for AOP in .NET Core
item.description: Aspect-oriented programming (AOP) decomposes an application’s source code into distinct aspects that isolate the core business logic from cross-cutting concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red Hat to acquire Neural Magic
item.description: Red Hat announced plans to acquire Neural Magic, a company that specializes in generative AI performance engineering, model optimization algorithms, and high-performance GPU and CPU inference serving.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
